message:
fix(manifest): improve cycle detection and simplify verified installer pipe

Two fixes to the manifest package generator:

1. DEPENDENCY CYCLE DETECTION (utils.ts - sortModulesByInstallOrder):
   - Changed from silent skip to throw Error on cycle detection
   - Error message includes the full dependency stack showing the cycle path
   - Example: "Dependency cycle detected: zsh depends on itself (directly
     or indirectly). Stack: base -> oh-my-zsh -> zsh -> zsh"
   - This prevents silent installation of modules in wrong order
   - Added debug console.log (commented out) for tracing visits
   - Fixed bug: visiting.delete(moduleId) was missing when module not found,
     which could cause false cycle detection on subsequent visits

2. VERIFIED INSTALLER PIPE SIMPLIFICATION (generate.ts - buildVerifiedInstallerPipe):
   - Removed special-case handling for non-bash/sh runners
   - The code block that quoted args for non-bash runners was never reached
     because the function only handles verified installers, which all use
     bash or sh as the runner
   - The remaining logic already handles the stdin piping case correctly
   - Simplifies the function and removes dead code

The cycle detection fix is important because the previous behavior would
silently skip cyclic dependencies, potentially leading to installation
failures that were hard to debug. Now the generator fails fast with a
clear error message pointing to the problematic dependency chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
